name: Presa de la Parra from Pontón de la Oliva — circular trailrunning through the Cañada de la Caleriza
name_original: Presa de la Parra desde Pontón de la Oliva — circular trailrunning por la Cañada de la Caleriza
description: This difficult 10.3-mile (16.6 km) circular trailrunning route from Pontón de la Oliva offers diverse terrain and historical sights along th
region_intro: Jogging routes around Uceda offer diverse terrain, from unique geological formations to expansive reservoir views. The region is characterized by the impressive Las Cárcavas, featuring eroded clay and sandstone landscapes with deep gorges. Nearby, the Embalse de El Atazar provides a network of trails with water views and a mountainous backdrop. This varied landscape makes Uceda suitable for different levels of running, including trail running. Best jogging routes around Uceda The most popular jogging route is Presa de la Parra from Pontón de la Oliva — circular trailrunning through the Cañada de la Caleriza, a 10.3 miles (16.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This circular route offers trail running through a distinctive natural area. Another top favourite among local runners is Cancho de la Cabeza Summit – Patones de Arriba loop from Patones de Abajo, a difficult 12.9 miles (20.8 km) path. This challenging loop includes a summit ascent and scenic views around Patones de Arriba. Local runners also love the Running loop from Torremocha de Jarama, a 6.2 miles (9.9 km) trail leading through the area around Torremocha de Jarama, often completed in about 1 hour 9 minutes. Jogging around Uceda is defined by unique geological formations like Las Cárcavas, vast reservoir views, and varied terrain. The network of routes offers options for easy jogs, moderate runs, and more difficult trail running challenges. The routes in Uceda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from a few reviews. More than 800 runners have used komoot to explore Uceda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Uceda?
A: Uceda offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 65 different trails to explore. These range from easy jogs to more challenging trail runs, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Uceda?
A: Yes, Uceda has several easy jogging routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. There are 5 routes specifically classified as easy, and many moderate options that can also be enjoyed at a gentler pace.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails in Uceda?
A: The running trails around Uceda offer diverse and striking scenery. You can expect to see unique geological formations like &lt;strong&gt;Las Cárcavas&lt;/strong&gt;, with their almost lunar landscape of deep gorges and barrancos. Many routes also provide expansive panoramic views of the &lt;strong&gt;Embalse de El Atazar&lt;/strong&gt;, the largest reservoir in the Community of Madrid, and its mountainous backdrop. You might also encounter historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón de la Oliva Dam&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Uceda area?
A: Yes, Uceda features several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031425963/presa-de-la-parra-from-ponton-de-la-oliva-circular-trailrunning-through-the-canada-de-la-caleriza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa de la Parra from Pontón de la Oliva&lt;/a&gt;, which offers trail running through a distinctive natural area. The region's varied terrain, especially around Las Cárcavas and El Atazar, is well-suited for creating scenic loops.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or points of interest I might see while running in Uceda?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can encounter several interesting landmarks. These include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho de la Cabeza Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6217974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atazar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4551346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Cárcavas - Pontón de la Oliva&lt;/a&gt; geological formations. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontón de la Oliva Dam and Cliffside Pathways&lt;/a&gt;, a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3407915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Suspension Bridge Over the Jarama River&lt;/a&gt;, and a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7420419" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the running trails in Uceda suitable for families?
A: While some routes in Uceda can be challenging, there are also easier trails that families can enjoy. Look for routes classified as 'easy' or 'moderate' with less elevation gain, particularly those around the Embalse de El Atazar which often feature wide, pleasant paths.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the jogging routes in Uceda?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many outdoor trails in Uceda. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural areas, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Please also be mindful of local regulations and clean up after your pet.

Q: Is parking available near the jogging routes in Uceda?
A: Yes, parking is typically available near popular trailheads. For instance, if you plan to explore the Las Cárcavas area or routes starting from Pontón de la Oliva, you'll usually find designated parking spots. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking information before your visit.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Uceda?
A: Uceda's varied terrain makes it suitable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late evening runs are recommended. Winter running is also possible, but some trails might be muddy or require appropriate gear depending on weather conditions.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Uceda?
A: The running community highly rates the trails in Uceda, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Runners often praise the unique geological formations of Las Cárcavas, the stunning panoramic views over the Embalse de El Atazar, and the diverse terrain that offers options for all skill levels, from easy jogs to challenging trail runs.

Q: How long are the running trails in Uceda typically?
A: The running trails in Uceda vary significantly in length. You can find shorter routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6929201" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Torremocha de Jarama&lt;/a&gt; which is about 6.2 miles (9.9 km), taking just over an hour. There are also much longer and more challenging routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31525552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Parra Dam and Weir – Cancho de la Cabeza loop from Valdepeñas de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 34 miles (55 km).

Q: Are there any challenging trail running options in Uceda?
A: Absolutely. Uceda is an excellent destination for challenging trail running. Nearly half of the routes (29 out of 65) are classified as difficult. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10417035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho de la Cabeza Summit – Patones de Arriba loop from Patones de Abajo&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 12.9-mile (20.8 km) path that includes a significant summit ascent and offers rewarding views.
tips: The La Parra dam was built with the purpose of relieving the Navarejos dam, which is located downstream of the Lozoya River. Due to more recent works, today the dam is in disuse but forms part of the well-known La Parra Dam - Pontón de la Oliva route.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trailrunning route?
faqs.answer: Based on komoot data, this trailrunning route typically takes around 2 hours and 3 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trailrunning route?
faqs.answer: The route features a diverse terrain. You'll encounter approximately 12.8 km of unpaved sections, natural paths, and some paved surfaces (around 157 m). It includes hiking paths (8.32 km) and general paths (7.13 km), with segments of single-track, forest track, and even some asphalted track. The paths along the Lozoya River are generally well-maintained.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered difficult for trailrunning due to its length (16.5 km) and significant elevation gain (370 meters up and down). It requires very good fitness and endurance. While some segments might be easier, the full circular route is a demanding run.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a mix of natural beauty and historical landmarks. You'll pass through the stunning Cañón del Río Lobos (GR-86) and by the historical La Parra Weir. You'll also see the Pontón de la Oliva dam, Madrid's oldest dam, and traverse areas with pine forests, riparian forests along the Lozoya River, and impressive limestone cliffs.

faqs.question: Where can I park for this trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, the trail starts from Pontón de la Oliva. It's advisable to research local parking options near the Pontón de la Oliva dam or the Cañada de la Caleriza trailhead before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the Presa de la Parra from Pontón de la Oliva trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other hikers, and to follow local regulations regarding pets in natural parks.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The scenery is particularly beautiful and varied. Late October is noted for its changing foliage, offering stunning autumn colors. Spring and early summer would also be excellent for milder temperatures and lush greenery. Always check weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for accessing this trailrunning route. It is generally open to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route often coincides with sections of other long-distance footpaths. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Senderos del Jarama, Senda 2: Remontando el Lozoya hasta la Presa de la Parra, GR 300 - Senda del Genaro, and the GR-86 - Cañada de la Caleriza, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife or plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich natural environment, especially along the Lozoya River, with lush riparian forests featuring species like ash and willow. You'll also pass through areas of jara (cistus) and monte bajo (low scrub), and pine forests. While specific wildlife sightings aren't guaranteed, the diverse habitat suggests opportunities to observe local fauna.

faqs.question: Are there any historical features to look out for?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The trail is rich in history. You'll encounter the Pontón de la Oliva dam, Madrid's first dam built in the mid-19th century, and the Presa de la Parra. You might also spot the historic 'minas de ataque' (attack mines), which are horizontal galleries that served the Canal de la Parra, showcasing impressive historical engineering.
